title: Python generated CLI completion promotion

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Promote Python #892 completion only when the clarified finish line is proven: generated Python owns parser/help/dispatch and codegen-owned primitive executors own generic deterministic file/data/schema/template/output behavior; remaining module-local runtime code is explicitly limited to package-specific judgment, live inspection, mutation orchestration, or thin target adapters.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep this branch Python-scoped. Do not advance TypeScript, Bash, PowerShell, or broad transport work. Do not claim full-generated-cli-complete until static proof can reject generic deterministic behavior hidden in accepted hand-owned runtime entries.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Tighten inventory semantics, migrate bounded representative generic operations to codegen-owned primitives, update proof gates, and route residual package-specific runtime judgment explicitly.
canonical_core.escalate_when: Completion requires inventing a broad DSL, changing non-Python target architecture, or moving live package judgment into generated code.
canonical_core.next_action: Make the Python operation execution inventory and generated-package proof distinguish codegen-owned generic primitive execution from accepted package-specific judgment, then promote current_state only if the proof honestly satisfies #892.
proof_expectations: uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Generated package static proof, Python Docker conformance, root CLI authority, closeout trust, package planning summary, and codegen-owned primitive executor conformance all agree that original Python generated-CLI intent is satisfied.

non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
intent.latitude: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: Make the Python operation execution inventory and generated-package proof distinguish codegen-owned generic primitive execution from accepted package-specific judgment, then promote current_state only if the proof honestly satisfies #892.
execution.proof: uv run python packages/command-generation/tests/primitive_conformance.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run pytest tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: planning.doctor.report and planning.report.report now execute generated operation resources through codegen-owned run_operation_steps while preserving planning payload sources and JSON/text compatibility formatting.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Migrating the remaining generic deterministic runtime-debt operations into codegen-owned primitive executors.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Generated operation resource packaging/classification scales mechanically for each runtime-consumed planning operation; planning report text compatibility currently remains package-specific formatting in the thin operation executor.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Migrate or retire the remaining generic-deterministic-runtime-debt entries, then rerun Python generated-package static, local conformance, Docker conformance, package tests/lint, contract tooling, structured inventory, and primitive conformance.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Move the memory deterministic report/doctor group behind codegen-owned primitive execution.

intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: The command-package IR can promote Python from adapter-layer-proven-not-full-generated-cli to full generated CLI completion only after parser, dispatch, interface, proof, runtime-handler boundaries, and codegen-owned generic primitive execution are all proven.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.tiny resumability note: The command-package IR can promote Python from adapter-layer-proven-not-full-generated-cli to full generated CLI completion only after parser, dispatch, interface, proof, runtime-handler boundaries, and codegen-owned generic primitive execution are all proven.
